commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR preserves terminal `chat.send` ACK status across Web, Android, Swift/native Talk, TUI, ACP, gateway Talk, slash-command forwarding, and Skill Workshop schema paths.

PR surface: Source +329, Tests +603, Other +615. Total +1547 across 31 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: the TUI `sendMessage` path records `/btw` and `/side` run ids, but the new terminal ACK cleanup executes only under `!isBtw`.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] One narrow mechanical TUI repair remains before the PR can be merge-ready.

target.title: fix: handle terminal chat send acknowledgements
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Preserves terminal `chat.send` ACK status (`ok`, `timeout`, `error`) through clients/helper paths that previously reduced the response to "has a run id".
- Clears, restores, or fails pending/optimistic state when the gateway has already returned a terminal ACK, so clients do not wait for run events that will not arrive.
- Covers Web queued/direct sends, detached `/btw`, queued `/steer`, `/redirect` via `sessions.steer`, Android chat/mic/Talk flows, native Talk flows, TUI, ACP, and the Skill Workshop forwarded schema.
- Updates Android chat, Android mic, and Swift chat timeout/error handling so terminal `timeout` / `error` ACKs surface failed-send state instead of looking accepted.

Why does this matter now?

Terminal ACKs are part of the `chat.send` contract. If a client treats one as an accepted active run, a message, mic turn, or queued command can get stuck or disappear without failure copy.

What is the intended outcome?

Clients branch on ACK status instead of only checking `runId`: active statuses keep the existing pending-run flow, while terminal statuses clear pending state, restore retryable queue/draft state where appropriate, or surface failure copy.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Redesigning the public SDK `Session.send` API shape.
- Standardizing every cross-client terminal-timeout message/toast policy.
- Broader lifecycle cleanup races not introduced by this PR.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Whether each touched consumer preserves `runId` and `status` until the final caller branches.
- Whether terminal `ok` / `timeout` / `error` branches clear or restore pending state without breaking non-terminal `started` / `in_flight` behavior.
- Whether Android chat/mic and Swift chat now surface terminal timeout/error as failed-send state.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #91048

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #84176
Related #84306

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No direct maintainer request; this follows the terminal ACK contract through additional consumers and addresses the Android terminal-timeout feedback.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed:
Terminal `chat.send` / forwarded ACKs with `status: "ok" | "timeout" | "error"` should not be treated as accepted active runs by Web, Android, TUI, ACP, gateway Talk, or slash-command consumers. The live proof exercises the widened terminal `timeout` path; final head `603380bfc4fbc1604ac16cb6915837191a36ad53` also includes Android chat, Android mic, and Swift chat timeout/error follow-up fixes.

- Real environment tested:
Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux using repository test runners, Android JVM/Robolectric, and a real local Gateway server + WebSocket RPC harness that drove the Control UI `sendChatMessage` controller source against a terminal `timeout` ACK. Hosted PR checks on final head `603380bfc4fbc1604ac16cb6915837191a36ad53` covered macOS Swift, Android build/test, lint, production/test types, and the repository real-behavior proof checker.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/gateway-protocol/src/schema/agents-models-skills.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/talk.test.ts src/tui/gateway-chat.test.ts src/tui/tui-command-handlers.test.ts src/acp/translator.lifecycle.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/slash-command-executor.node.test.ts
cd apps/android && ANDROID_HOME=/opt/android-sdk ANDROID_SDK_ROOT=/opt/android-sdk ./gradlew :app:testPlayDebugUnitTest --tests ai.openclaw.app.gateway.ChatSendAckTest --tests ai.openclaw.app.chat.ChatControllerTerminalAckTest --tests ai.openclaw.app.voice.MicCaptureManagerTest
pnpm tsgo:core && pnpm tsgo:test:ui && pnpm tsgo:test:packages
git diff --check

# Temporary uncommitted live proof harness copied to src/gateway/pr91049-live-terminal-ack-proof.test.ts, then removed.
PR91049_HEAD=c18e743ba4ced535ec5351ba536a542db3d7621f node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/pr91049-live-terminal-ack-proof.test.ts
```

The final follow-up commit from `c18e743ba4ced535ec5351ba536a542db3d7621f` to `603380bfc4fbc1604ac16cb6915837191a36ad53` changed Android and Swift client files only; it did not change the Gateway/Control UI path covered by the live proof above.

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

Focused suite output from this branch:

```text
Test Files 9 passed (9)
Tests 237 passed (237)
Android JVM focused ACK suites: BUILD SUCCESSFUL
Type checks: pnpm tsgo:core, tsgo:test:ui, tsgo:test:packages passed
Touched TypeScript oxlint: Found 0 warnings and 0 errors
```

Live Gateway + Control UI terminal-ACK proof from head `c18e743ba4ced535ec5351ba536a542db3d7621f` for the unchanged Gateway/Control UI path:

```text
✓ gateway-core ../../src/gateway/pr91049-live-terminal-ack-proof.test.ts (1 test)
✓ gateway-server ../../src/gateway/pr91049-live-terminal-ack-proof.test.ts (1 test)
✓ gateway-client ../../src/gateway/pr91049-live-terminal-ack-proof.test.ts (1 test)
Test Files 3 passed (3)
Tests 3 passed (3)
```

Copied proof artifact from that run:

```json
{
  "proofKind": "real Gateway server + WebSocket RPC + Control UI sendChatMessage state cleanup",
  "branchHead": "c18e743ba4ced535ec5351ba536a542db3d7621f",
  "scenario": {
    "terminalStatus": "timeout",
    "gatewayTerminalAck": { "runId": "00000000-0000-4000-8000-000000091049", "status": "timeout", "summary": "aborted" },
    "uiResult": null,
    "uiStateAfter": {
      "chatSending": false,
      "chatRunId": null,
      "chatStream": null,
      "chatMessagesLength": 0,
      "lastError": "The run ended before the message was accepted."
    }
  }
}
```

Hosted PR checks on final head `603380bfc4fbc1604ac16cb6915837191a36ad53`:

- `CI / macos-swift`: `SUCCESS` (https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/27089311492/job/79949792011)
- `CI / android-build-play`: `SUCCESS` (https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/27089311492/job/79949792040)
- `CI / android-test-play`: `SUCCESS` (https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/27089311492/job/79949792010)
- `CI / android-test-third-party`: `SUCCESS` (https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/27089311492/job/79949792018)
- `CI / check-lint`: `SUCCESS` (https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/27089311492/job/79949792027)
- `Real behavior proof / Real behavior proof`: `SUCCESS` (https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/27089323348/job/79949789316)
- `Auto response / auto-response`: `SUCCESS` (https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/27089323350/job/79949790359)

- Observed result after fix:
The focused suites cover terminal ACK handling for protocol schema, gateway Talk, TUI, ACP, slash-command forwarding, Android chat, and Android mic ACK handling. The live Gateway proof shows a terminal `timeout` ACK returned over WebSocket to the Control UI `sendChatMessage` path; after the ACK, local pending state is cleared (`chatSending:false`, `chatRunId:null`, `chatStream:null`), the optimistic message is removed, and user-visible error state is set instead of leaving a stuck pending run. Hosted checks on the final head show macOS Swift, Android, lint, production/test types, and the real-behavior proof checker succeeded.

- What was not tested:
Full browser/device manual UI testing was not performed. The live proof exercises the Control UI controller source and Gateway WebSocket transport, not a screenshot-driven browser session.

- Proof limitations or environment constraints:
The live proof controls the agent reply boundary through the repository Gateway test seam so `chat.abort` can deterministically produce a terminal `timeout` ACK. Gateway transport, idempotency caching, terminal ACK payload, and the Control UI controller cleanup path are real source code. The temporary proof harness was not committed to the feature branch. Swift execution was validated by hosted `macos-swift`, not by the local Linux checkout.

- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):
Focused RED tests were used during development for Web helper callers, Android chat timeout/error acceptance, Android mic terminal timeout, ACP terminal `ok`, and `/redirect` terminal ACK handling before the fixes were applied.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- Focused JS suite listed in the Real behavior proof section.
- Focused Android JVM/Robolectric ACK suites for `ChatSendAckTest`, `ChatControllerTerminalAckTest`, and `MicCaptureManagerTest`.
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:ui`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:packages`
- Touched TypeScript oxlint.
- `git diff --check`
- Hosted PR checks listed above on final head `603380bfc4fbc1604ac16cb6915837191a36ad53`.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Web direct send, queued send, detached `/btw`, queued `/steer`, and `/redirect` terminal ACK behavior.
- Android ACK parsing, chat controller terminal ACK handling, and mic terminal ACK handling.
- Swift chat terminal timeout feedback.
- Gateway Talk terminal ACK rejection before realtime subscription.
- ACP terminal `ok` parity with terminal timeout/error.
- TUI terminal ACK send-result handling.
- Skill Workshop forwarded ACK schema accepting the widened terminal statuses.

What failed before this fix, if known?

RED coverage showed terminal ACK helper callers, Android chat, and Android mic handling could still collapse, accept, or discard terminal statuses after the initial propagation work. `/redirect` also treated terminal forwarded ACKs as a successful redirect with a tracked run id.

If no test was added, why not?

Tests were added or updated for the main status-preserving branches. Swift source changes include test updates; hosted `macos-swift` passed on the final head.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Clients now clear, restore, or fail pending state when the gateway returns a terminal ACK instead of waiting for future run events.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Session/chat lifecycle state: clearing pending or optimistic state too early could hide a message, while not clearing it leaves clients stuck.

How is that risk mitigated?

The patch preserves non-terminal `started` / `in_flight` behavior, keeps `runId` remapping where needed, restores retryable draft/queue state on terminal failure, and adds regression coverage for the changed branches.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Review the status-preserving ACK branches and the Android/Swift terminal-timeout follow-up on final head `603380bfc4fbc1604ac16cb6915837191a36ad53`.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- Hosted PR checks on the final head are green/neutral-only at the time of this update.
- The real-behavior proof gate and platform checks have completed successfully on the final head.
- This PR is waiting on final bot/maintainer review.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

The Android terminal-timeout feedback was addressed by making terminal timeout/error ACKs surface failed acceptance in Android chat, adding focused Android chat regressions, and covering sibling Android mic and Swift chat timeout/error behavior.
